Curtin Research Express Interest
Now Recruiting

A New Treatment for OCD — Join Our Research Trial

Curtin University is seeking adults who experience obsessive-compulsive disorder to participate in a funded clinical research study.

Check If I'm Eligible Learn More

About the Study

This study is run by researchers at Curtin University and aims to evaluate a structured psychological treatment for obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD). We are investigating whether this approach can help reduce OCD symptoms and improve daily functioning.

Participation involves attending sessions with a trained clinician over several weeks. The treatment is evidence-based, and all sessions are provided free of charge to eligible participants.

All treatment sessions are provided at no cost to participants. You may also receive a small reimbursement for your time.

Who Can Participate?

You may be eligible if you meet the following criteria:

🗓️

Age 18 or older

The study is open to adults aged 18 and above.

🧠

Experiencing OCD symptoms

You currently experience obsessions, compulsions, or both that affect your daily life.

📍

Located in Perth, WA

Able to attend in-person sessions at the Curtin University campus in Bentley.

🌏

Fluent in English

Sessions and assessments are conducted in English.

What's Involved?

Here is what you can expect if you decide to participate:

  • 1

    Express Interest

    Fill in a short online form below. This takes about 5 minutes and does not commit you to anything.

  • 2

    Screening Call

    A researcher will contact you for a brief phone screen to confirm eligibility.

  • 3

    Baseline Assessment

    You will complete a structured interview and questionnaires to assess current symptoms.

  • 4

    Treatment Sessions

    Weekly sessions with a clinician over the course of the trial program.

  • 5

    Follow-up Assessment

    Brief check-ins at the end of treatment and at follow-up to measure outcomes.

Ready to Find Out More?

Express your interest and a member of our research team will be in touch within a few business days.

Email Us to Apply
Or contact us directly: ocd.trial@curtin.edu.au  ·  School of Population Health, Curtin University
